title: Poolside Chromatic Light Lab
description: An iridescent lamp hovers above a pool, casting colored caustic-like light onto the water surface through post-processing effects. The user adjusts bloom intensity, chromatic aberration, tone mapping, and color grading parameters across multiple lamp instances to achieve a target visual state, with undo/redo support and a conservation rule that the total light energy across all lamps must remain constant.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with EffectComposer post-processing. Load pool.glb centered at origin and place three instances of IridescenceLamp.glb hovering 2 units above the pool at positions (-2,2,0), (0,2,1.5), and (2,2,0). Use a perspective camera at (0,5,7) looking at the pool center. Add ambient light (0.15 intensity) and one directional light. Set up EffectComposer with RenderPass, UnrealBloomPass (threshold 0.2, strength 0.5, radius 0.4), and a custom ShaderPass for chromatic aberration (uniform offset 0.0-0.02) and color grading (RGB gain uniforms, range 0.5-1.5). Each lamp has individual properties: bloom contribution (0-1), chromatic aberration spread (0-0.02), and color tint (RGB sliders 0.5-1.5). Implement a CONSERVATION LAW: the sum of bloom contributions across all three lamps must always equal 1.5. When the user increases one lamp's bloom, the others decrease proportionally. Create a left-side panel with collapsible sections per lamp showing sliders for bloom contribution, chromatic aberration, and RGB color tint. Add global sliders for tone mapping exposure (0.5-3.0) and overall bloom radius. Implement full undo/redo with Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y tracking all slider changes as discrete states (store snapshots). Each lamp should have a visible PointLight child whose color and intensity update reactively with the sliders. The pool material should have envMapIntensity adjustable via a global slider. Show a small HUD displaying current total bloom energy to verify conservation. The target interaction flow is: user adjusts individual lamp blooms (auto-balanced), tweaks chromatic aberration per lamp, adjusts color grading, changes tone mapping, uses undo to revert mistakes, and fine-tunes the global bloom radius — totaling 10-14 meaningful steps. Use OrbitControls for camera navigation.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  lamp1BloomContribution: number,         // bloom contribution of first lamp (0-1)
  lamp2BloomContribution: number,         // bloom contribution of second lamp (0-1)
  lamp3BloomContribution: number,         // bloom contribution of third lamp (0-1)
  lamp1ChromaticOffset: number,         // chromatic aberration offset for first lamp (0-0.02)
  lamp2ChromaticOffset: number,         // chromatic aberration offset for second lamp (0-0.02)
  lamp3ChromaticOffset: number,         // chromatic aberration offset for third lamp (0-0.02)
  lamp1ColorTintR: number,         // red color tint for first lamp (0.5-1.5)
  lamp1ColorTintG: number,         // green color tint for first lamp (0.5-1.5)
  lamp1ColorTintB: number,         // blue color tint for first lamp (0.5-1.5)
  globalBloomRadius: number,         // overall bloom radius parameter (0.1-1.0)
  toneMappingExposure: number,         // tone mapping exposure value (0.5-3.0)
  undoStackDepth: number,         // number of states in undo history
}
